zh.lifeSummary: 當Otto von Are於大約1122年出生在德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 阿維利阿爾騰亞爾時，他的父親Theodoricus II. von Are11120歲，母親Mathilde von Sponheim32歲。他和Adelheid Von Hochstaden至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1167年享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Are
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÍr or Ó hÉir ‘descendant of Ír’, a personal name, possibly meaning ‘long-lasting’, borne by a legendary ancestor of the north of Ireland. This name was always monosyllabic.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAichir ‘descendant of Aichear’, a personal name derived from the epithet aichear ‘fierce, sharp’. The personal name on which this surname is based was originally disyllabic. Although the Anglicized forms O'Hehir and Hehir still exist, particularly in Ireland, pronunciation in later northern Irish has caused Ó hAichir to fall together with another surname, based on a one-syllable personal name, as in sense 1 above.

English: nickname from Middle English hare, harr, here ‘hare’ (Old English hara, sometimes influenced by Old Norse heri). It may have denoted someone who could run fast or was timorous, or who bore some similarity to a hare in appearance, such as bulging eyes.

variants.name: Hehir
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brighid.
Irish (Clare): variant of Hare 2 from Ó hAichir.

variants.name: Hairr
meanings: Irish and English: variant of   Hare   or   Hair  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German   Herr  .

variants.name: Haire
meanings: Irish (northern): variant of   Hare   or   O'Hare  .

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

en.lifeSummary: When Otto von Are was born about 1122, in Altenahr, Ahrweiler, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, his father, Theodoricus II. von Are, was 11120 and his mother, Mathilde von Sponheim, was 32. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Adelheid Von Hochstaden. He died in 1167, at the age of 46.

ko.lifeSummary: Otto von Are(이)가 대략 1122년 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 랜드크레이스 아h위러 알텐아h르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Theodoricus II. von Are(은)는 11120세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde von Sponheim(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Adelheid Von Hochstaden(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1167년 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otto von Are родился около 1122 г. в местности алтенар, ландкрис ахрвилир, раинланд-пфалз, Германия, его отцу по имени Theodoricus II. von Are было 11120 лет, а матери, которую звали Mathilde von Sponheim, 32. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Adelheid Von Hochstaden. Он умер в 1167 г. в возрасте 46 лет.
